target: data/sections.json:part4.html Section 4.6 (Registry Framing Audit) — fix prose-data mismatch + add Currently-classified-claims mirror sub-table; defer wins.json schema extension as superseded
source_iss_routing_reason: EXP-183 E_PRIMARY schema deferred. Coupled with ISS-1130 cluster. Resolve as part of EXP-183 re-evaluation. [Re-routed from pending-human 2026-05-10: PROP-026 M1 matrix lacks route-to-analyst path]
review_class_rationale: New sub-table is fresh content (per-claim mirror of dome's current claim_index.json); the prose-correction is mechanical but the decision to *scope-correct rather than implement schema* is a judgment call the curmudgeon should attack freshly. Author guidance per PROP-025: when in doubt, declare deep-attack.
kernel_of_truth: The original EXP-183 E_PRIMARY schema proposal was substantively well-designed — extending data/wins.json with five author_* fields would have given us a natural-expansion canary: if the dome author classified more claims, our WIN cards would auto-update. The proposal correctly identified the audit-monitor value of per-WIN visibility. What changed under us is the dome's API itself: between 2026-04-18 (EXP-183 author date) and 2026-05-16 (this audit), the dome refactored claim_index.json — `entries` → `claims`, removed `strict_prospective_confirmed`, replaced `registry_status` with `status`. Two of the five proposed fields no longer have a source-of-truth in the dome's API. EXP-183's framing is correct; its specific schema list is partially stale.
problem_discovered: Section 4.6 (Registry Framing Audit) text was integrated on 2026-04-18 from EXP-183. The text claims — three times — that per-WIN author_* fields exist in our WIN cards: (1) 'those live on the individual WIN detail cards under the new Author's Self-Rating block'; (2) 'Our WIN cards now carry an author_claim_class field per WIN (defaulting to ...)'; (3) 'See also: per-WIN author self-rating fields (new in data/wins.json schema 2026-04-18)'. NONE of these are true. data/wins.json has zero author_* fields on any of 71 WINs (verified via `grep author_ data/wins.json` returns nothing). The prose got integrated; the data work was deferred (becoming ISS-1147); the deferred work was never completed; the prose was never reconciled. This is a credibility hole — we criticize the dome for publishing claims its data doesn't back up, and we are doing the same thing on the very section where we lay out that critique.
what_changed_since_iss_filing: ISS-1147 was filed 2026-04-18 as 'low-risk additive change' deferred E_PRIMARY schema extension. Severity=minor.

Between 2026-04-18 and 2026-05-16, the dome's claim_index.json was refactored. As fetched today (2026-05-16) from https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json: top-level structure now uses `claims` (was `entries`), removed `strict_prospective_confirmed` field entirely, replaced `registry_status` with `status`. Two of EXP-183's five proposed fields (`author_strict_prospective_confirmed`, `author_registry_status`) no longer have a source.

Same 9 classified items present in dome's claim_index.json: win-069-australia-scaffold, win-068-eclipse-ensemble, win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-058-theta-rule, win-011-mohe-contact-phase, win-056-solar-elevation, win-062-tesla-wave-speed, pred-eclipse-tier3, pred-solar-009. Class distribution unchanged: 4 retrospective_structural, 2 pending_contested, 1 supportive_nonunique, 1 prospective_confirmed, 1 open_refinement. registry_integrity_notes still flags WIN-058 (duplicate-label) and WIN-062 (duplicate Tesla blocks); WIN-058b is no longer separately listed (now disambiguated as win-058-theta-rule).
Section 4.6 already integrated (per ISS-1147 notes). Its text references per-WIN cards that don't exist.

EXP-396 (2026-05-16 prior run) just patched Row 2 of the same gap-audit-table to update count drift to 10/12/1. These two patches are independent edits to the same section; EXP-397's find-and-replace anchors do not overlap with EXP-396's.

evidence_collected_this_run.dome_claim_index_last_updated_field: 2026-04-17 (unchanged — but structure has changed under that timestamp)

decision: SCOPE CORRECTION (Option B + minimal mirror table), not full implementation. Three reasons: (1) the dome's API has changed under EXP-183's proposed schema — two fields are now unsourced; (2) the prose-data mismatch is the urgent correctness problem and is fixable in 4 patches without touching wins.json; (3) the analytical value of per-WIN cards is captured by a single sub-table within Section 4.6 listing the 9 currently-classified entries — readers see the catalog without 60 empty stubs on 60 WIN cards.
decision_counter_argument: The other side of the argument: maybe we SHOULD do the full implementation. Pro-implementation case: (a) the audit-monitor framing is strongest when the WIN cards themselves update — per-WIN visibility is where readers already are; (b) 60 empty stubs is not fatal — EXP-183 already specified collapsed-stub rendering when all author_* fields are null; (c) the renaming/refactor in the dome's API is exactly the kind of churn this audit should make visible to readers, and a fully-implemented schema would surface that churn automatically over time. We considered this and chose scope-correction anyway because: the dome's API has churned twice in 30 days (the count fix from EXP-396, the claim_index restructure detected today), and the schema we'd add now would itself need follow-up patches each time. A sub-table mirror is easier to update than a per-WIN render pipeline.

patches.find: Per-WIN divergences (WIN-058 "pending independent verification", WIN-058/058b/062 registry_integrity_notes, and the 5-class claim taxonomy where it's applied) are not listed here — those live on the individual WIN detail cards under the new Author's Self-Rating block, so side-by-side comparison is the default where people already read our verdicts.
patches.replace: Per-WIN divergences (WIN-058 "pending independent verification" status, the WIN-058 and WIN-062 duplicate-label registry_integrity_notes, and the 5-class claim taxonomy where it has been applied) are listed in the Currently classified claims sub-table below — a direct mirror of the dome's api/current/claim_index.json as of the date in the sub-table heading.
patches.rationale: The original claim that divergences 'live on the individual WIN detail cards under the new Author's Self-Rating block' is false — no such block exists. Replace with accurate description: divergences are listed in the sub-table within this section.

patches.replace: The classified entries are mirrored in the Currently classified claims sub-table below; new classifications land there on the next analyst refresh.
patches.rationale: The original claim 'Our WIN cards now carry an author_claim_class field per WIN' is false — wins.json has zero author_* fields. Replace with accurate description: classifications are mirrored in the sub-table.

Schema-drift note. An earlier audit (2026-04-18) proposed extending data/wins.json with five author_* fields and rendering an inline Author's Self-Rating block on every WIN card. Between then and this refresh, the dome's claim_index.json was restructured (entries → claims; strict_prospective_confirmed removed; registry_status → status). Two of the five proposed fields no longer have a source in the dome's API. We mirror the current taxonomy in this sub-table rather than carry 60+ null-default fields on WIN rows — the analytical thrust (visible-layer audit lag) is preserved; the implementation is leaner.

Kernel of truth — what the API layer gets right

Anchor is unique within part4.html (verified one match). The replacement preserves the Kernel-of-truth h3 line untouched at the end.

holes_addressed: ISS-1147 deferred E_PRIMARY schema work: resolved as superseded — implementation is leaner sub-table instead of full per-WIN schema. Decider should close ISS-1147 with resolution 'superseded by EXP-397 scope correction'.
Prose-data mismatch in Section 4.6 (false claims about per-WIN cards): three false claims patched.
Source-data churn since 2026-04-18: schema-drift note documents the refactor and explains why the original proposal isn't a clean fit anymore.

Dome's claim_index.json structural changes since 2026-04-18 (entries → claims, removed strict_prospective_confirmed, registry_status → status) — documented for the schema-drift note in the new sub-table.

anticipated_objections.objection: You're shrinking from a substantive proposal. The full schema would be more rigorous.
anticipated_objections.response: The full schema would be more rigorous only if the dome's API were stable. Two of the five fields the schema proposed are already stale — that's a 40% staleness rate after 28 days. Adding a per-WIN render pipeline that depends on fields the dome is actively retiring is anti-rigor: it creates a maintenance liability without proportionate audit value. The sub-table approach preserves the analytical thrust (visible mirror of the author's classification surface) with one update path instead of three (wins.json fields + generate-html.js template + per-WIN card CSS).

anticipated_objections.objection: You're closing an issue without doing the work. ISS-1147 was filed; this looks like task avoidance.
anticipated_objections.response: The deferred work the issue tracks no longer maps cleanly onto the source API. Closing as 'superseded' rather than 'done' is the honest call. The substantive correctness fix — patching the false prose claims and adding the mirror sub-table — is being done in this same EXP. We don't carry the original implementation as if it were still scoped; we update the scope and resolve.

anticipated_objections.objection: The sub-table will go stale the next time the dome edits claim_index.json.
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — and that staleness is the audit-monitor feature, not a bug. When the dome adds a 10th classified claim, our sub-table will be one row short, and the next analyst refresh will close that gap. Same monitoring property as the per-WIN-render approach, with one update path instead of three.

anticipated_objections.objection: If the sub-table is fine, the original EXP-183 was overengineered. That's a process critique of the prior analyst run.
anticipated_objections.response: Partially true. EXP-183 was a well-intentioned response to HNOTE-API-HUMAN-GAPS-001 that converged on the per-WIN-card pattern because it puts the audit where readers already are. The pattern is fine in principle; the cost-benefit shifts when the source API is moving. The lesson for the pipeline: when proposing a multi-file schema extension that depends on a third-party API, build in a staleness check before declaring 'integrated'. EXP-183's Step-1 backfill task was never fully scheduled — that's the gap the integrity agent should flag.

summary_for_decider: ISS-1147 (deferred EXP-183 schema extension) cannot be cleanly implemented because the dome's claim_index.json API has been refactored since EXP-183 was authored (two of five proposed fields no longer have source data). More urgently, Section 4.6 prose currently claims per-WIN author_* fields exist — they don't. EXP-397 patches the three false prose claims and adds a 9-row sub-table mirroring the dome's current claim_index.json directly inside Section 4.6. Recommend closing ISS-1147 with resolution 'superseded by EXP-397 scope correction' rather than 'fixed'. Honest classification: this is a scope reduction, not full implementation. The audit-monitor property (visible mirror of the author's classification surface) is preserved with a leaner update path. Patches: 4 find-and-replace operations on data/sections.json:part4.html — three correct false prose claims, one inserts the new sub-table before the existing Kernel-of-truth h3. All anchors verified unique in part4.html. Tests must run before commit per CLAUDE.md (Critical Workflow Rule: Run Tests Before Committing Data Changes).

recommended_action_iss_1147.close_reason: EXP-183 schema fields (author_registry_status, author_strict_prospective_confirmed) no longer have source data in dome's claim_index.json post-refactor. EXP-397 supplies the analytical artifact (per-claim mirror sub-table inside Section 4.6) without the per-WIN render pipeline.

out_of_scope_observations_for_decider: The integrity agent should run a check that scans integrated section prose for references to data fields that don't exist in data/wins.json or data/sections.json. The 'Our WIN cards now carry an author_claim_class field' claim sat in production for ~28 days; that kind of prose-data mismatch is exactly what a static integrity rule could catch.

Section 4.6 still references WIN-058b separately in the See-also footer (EXP-181 per-WIN patches), but the dome's current claim_index.json no longer lists WIN-058b — it's been disambiguated as win-058-theta-rule. Not patching this footer reference in EXP-397 because it points to OUR EXP-181 work (which used the older label), and breaking that internal pointer would lose history. Possible future scope: add a note next to the WIN-058b reference clarifying that the dome's API now disambiguates the two claims under WIN-058.

EXP-183 itself remains as the authoritative scope document for the registry-level subsection (F_PRIMARY_REGISTRY_SUBSECTION). That subsection IS integrated and stands. Only the E_PRIMARY schema extension is being closed-as-superseded.
newsworthy_rationale: Internal scope correction; no headline-grade dome-author signal here. The dome's claim_index.json refactor is interesting but happened quietly under an unchanged last_updated field — possibly worth a poller note rather than a Breaking News item.
tldr_patch_note: Section 4.6 already has a TLDR; the patches don't change its thrust (visible/API divergence audit). The TLDR mentions 'individual WIN cards' implicitly via the gap-audit pattern but doesn't promise per-WIN rendering — so it stays accurate after the four patches.

other_side_caveats.concern: Closing ISS-1147 as superseded might be seen as ducking commitments. The original EXP-183 was approved and integrated; the deferred work was a sub-task of that approval.
other_side_caveats.mitigation: EXP-397 explicitly acknowledges this in the kernel-of-truth and anticipated-objections fields. The pipeline's audit trail (this EXP file + the decider's close action) makes the scope-reduction visible. We are not pretending the work was done; we are documenting why a leaner artifact does the same job.

other_side_caveats.concern: If the dome author classifies more claims in the next month, the sub-table needs an update path. Without explicit poller wiring, that update is manual.
other_side_caveats.mitigation: The poller's daily check should diff dome's api/current/claim_index.json.claims[] against this sub-table. If the count or any per-claim claim_class/status changes, the poller should write an attention-inbox item for the analyst. Adding this poller wiring is out of scope for EXP-397 — flagged as a follow-up for the tinker.
